This workshop will provide technical knowledge and insights within the context of imaging infection and focus on host-pathogen/parasite interactions, including hands-on experience of sample preparation for live cell imaging, fundamentals of optics, and fluorescent imaging probes. The funds will go towards providing teaching faculty and student accommodation and travel to the University of Cape Town where the workshop is being held.

In support of the Conference for Undergraduate Women in Physics to be held January 17-19, 2020 in Chicago, Illinois. The primary goal of the CUWiP conference is to help undergraduate women continue in physics by providing them with the opportunity to experience a professional conference, information about graduate school and professions in physics, and access to other women in physics of all ages with whom they can share experiences, advice, and ideas. The funds from contribution will go towards housing and meals and other conference costs.

GrantOrContriApprvForFutGrp.GrantOrContributionPurposeTxt: In support of theoretical investigations of emergent phenomena in quantum materials.
In support of scientific research by Dr. Cui-Zu Chang, improving synthesis methods for high-temperature quantum anomalous Hall (QAH) insulators and QAH/superconductor hybrid structures.
To support small grants in British Columbia.
To support a training program to advance civil society's understanding of MapaInversiones-based platforms.

GrantOrContriApprvForFutGrp.GrantOrContributionPurposeTxt: In support of an international research effort to monitor volcanoes in real time.

RecipientBusinessName.BusinessNameLine1Txt: Trustees of Columbia University in the City of NY

GrantOrContriApprvForFutGrp.GrantOrContributionPurposeTxt: In support of Pangeo: a collection of open source software tools to advance knowledge in the climate sciences.

In support of scientific research by Dr. Robert Cava to explore the "molecules in metals" model for magnetic and electronic materials, new superconducting high entropy alloys, and the hydrothermal method for iridate growth.

In support of the continued development of ASPIRE (Algorithms for Single Particle Reconstruction), an emerging software package for three-dimensional structure determination of macromolecules by cryo-electron microscopy.
In support of scientific research by Dr. Leslie Schoop to explore the synthesis of layered magnetic materials, correlated topological materials, and novel two-dimensional materials.
In support of theoretical investigations of emergent phenomena in quantum materials.
In support of research that unites biophysics, quantum optics theory and nanophotonic technology to obtain signatures of quantum mechanical effects in single biomolecules.

In support of research to develop a novel multiview computational imaging system comprised of algorithms applied to a line-scanning, superresolution, aberration-corrected microscope to answer fundamental questions in animal development.
To support the development of shared data resources and machine learning algorithms at Contra Costa County Health Services to improve clinical diagnosis.

To support the engagement of youth and educators with the scientific discovery process taking place at observatories on Maunakea through a collaborative naming program, with accompanying curriculum, exhibits and planetarium programming.
To support the study of propulsion in marine animals that use multiple fluidic jets to move.

In support of scientific research by Dr. David Mandrus on quantum spin liquid materials, the spintronic functionalities of magnetic Weyl semimetals, and tuning magnetic states of materials with electrical currents.

In support of scientific research by Dr. Johnpierre Paglione aiming to improve the protocols and techniques for synthesizing quantum materials and accelerate materials discovery via machine learning techniques.
In support of operationalizing annual high-resolution crop monitoring across South America using automatic classification.

RecipientBusinessName.BusinessNameLine1Txt: Virginia Polytechnic Institute and State Univ

GrantOrContriApprvForFutGrp.GrantOrContributionPurposeTxt: In support of developing a new method that uses ultrasound technology to extract DNA from wood tissue.
To support the protection of freshwater ecosystems in the Maranon Basin of Peru.
In support of a data-driven system that turns crowdsourced photographs and videos into information about wildlife.
To support innovation in understanding free-flowing rivers and citizen science discovery.
To expand the Loreto Regional Conservation System (RCS) in Peru to 6.9 million hectares by applying improved and inclusive management effectiveness tools.
To support progress toward achieving the consolidation targets for protected areas and indigenous lands in two high biodiversity mosaics in Bolivia, Peru and Ecuador (6.8 million hectares).
To secure financial sustainability for 25.2 million hectares of new and existing protected areas in Colombia, including 12 Amazonian conservation areas (about 8.3 million hectares).
To support the development of an integrated system that uses acoustic techniques to enable multi-vehicle swarming underwater.

In support of research to develop an Arctic Carbon Monitoring and Prediction System that will incorporate ground based, airborne and satellite remote sensing data and computer modeling into a policy framework.
In support of the Tropical Forest Alliance.
To create the enabling conditions for enhanced forest and land use monitoring and transparency to achieve conversion free commodity production in the Chaco.
In support of Phase I of the New Climate Economy Initiative.
To ensure a strong framework for the protection and sustainable use of Canada's Eastern Arctic Ocean.
To support the implementation of innovative infrastructure guidelines in the Colombian Amazon.
To secure financial sustainability for 25.2 million hectares of new and existing protected areas in Colombia, including 12 Amazonian conservation areas (about 8.3 million hectares).
To develop accounting guidance for greenhouse gas emissions related to land use under the Greenhouse Gas Protocol.

To support scientific research by Dr. Judy Cha, including development of in situ probes for monitoring materials growth by chemical vapor deposition as well as development of scalable fabrication processes for nanostructure-based quantum materials.

In support of scientific research by Dr. Ivan Bozovic on the synthesis of cuprate superconductors, two-dimensional topological quantum materials, and metal hydrides by atomic-layer-by-layer molecular beam epitaxy.

GeneralExplanationGrp.MediumExplanationTxt: FORM 990PF, PART I, LINE 18 - TAXES CURRENT EXCISE TAX 4,383,746 DEFERRED EXCISE TAX 19,109,212 UNRELATED BUSINESS INCOME TAX -654,668 ========== 22,838,290

FORM 990PF, PART IX-A, LINE 1 - SUMMARY OF DIRECT CHARITABLE ACTIVITIES IN ADDITION TO ITS GRANTMAKING, THE FOUNDATION ENGAGES DIRECTLY IN NUMEROUS ACTIVITIES TO SUPPORT OUR PROGRAM AREAS AND THE PHILANTHROPIC SECTOR IN GENERAL. DIRECT CHARITABLE EXPENSES IN THE AMOUNT OF $3,693,833 INCLUDE THE FOLLOWING ACTIVITIES: * Convening groups of experts, other foundations, and grantees to discuss and disseminate information about policy, research and trends * Supporting Foundation staff in their leadership roles in their respective fields, including service on boards and sharing their expertise with others * Providing technical assistance to grantees and other partners involved in our programmatic activities

FORM 990PF, PART IX, LINE 4 - Recoveries of Qualifying Distributions Included in Line 4 is an adjustment of $252,430 related to the 2015 Amended FOrm 990-T and the reduction of UBTI tax liability. The reduction in UBTI tax results in an increase to the 2019 distributable amount. Per guidance and communications from the Internal Revenue Service, the adjustment from the 2015 Form 990-T was to be made on the next filed return.
